title: Silky Citrus Venezuelan Beef Curry
description: A fragrant, citrusinfused beef curry with a silky coconutcashew cream, bright lime and orange notes, and a smoky bhuna base, perfect for a romantic dinner.

ingredients.name: Coconut cashew sauce
ingredients.amount: 400ml coconut milk + 50g soaked cashews blended with 120ml water

instructions: Soak cashews in water for 20 minutes. Drain.
In a blender, combine soaked cashews, 400 ml coconut milk, and 120 ml water. Blend until silky smooth; set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large, deep skillet over medium‑high heat.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger; sauté until onion is translucent and fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Stir in ground cumin, ground coriander, and smoked paprika; toast for 1 minute until spices are aromatic.
Add beef cubes to the skillet; sear on all sides until browned, about 5‑7 minutes.
Sprinkle with lime zest, orange zest, salt, and pepper; stir to coat the beef and aromatics.
Pour in the cashew‑coconut sauce, then add lime juice and orange juice; bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let the curry slow‑cook for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beef is tender and the sauce thickens.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve hot, garnished with lime wedges and chopped cilantro if desired, over cauliflower rice or plain rice.
